runner.measurement: fresh child processes; canonical candidate resolution precedes the timed analysis stage; Linux peak RSS from /usr/bin/time -f %M

cold.rationale: Phase Z source 6fa5079 CI run 29649631288 measured 23,315.693 ms and 24,568.744 ms on unchanged-head attempts. The frozen 30,000 ms ceiling retains 22.11% headroom over the worse attempt, above the observed 5.37% attempt variance.

canonicalResolvedAnalysis.rationale: Z07 replaces the retired lexical analyzeChange metric with canonical resolved-facts analysis over one changed candidate. Resolution and the validated public oracle are outside the timed stage. After the local 20.851 ms recording, three comparable Linux Node 20 distributions measured 84.023, 81.066, and 84.568 ms p95 with exact byte, verdict, facts, tree, and changed-identity parity. The reviewed 100 ms ceiling retains 18.25% headroom over the final/worst comparable p95; the earlier 50 ms target was retired before arming because it was unreachable on the recorded runner without changing the endpoint or caching a verdict.

oneShotWarm.rationale: Z04 retired the pre-resolved-facts semantic cache because it could preserve stale or divergent verdicts. The one-shot warm control must remain cache-free, byte-identical to cold JSON, and no slower than the frozen 30,000 ms cold ceiling until Z07 supplies an identity-keyed snapshot.

memory.rationale: Z07 source d5cbe61 / PR synthetic candidate 381e51b, CI run 29704311754 measured a 723,099,648-byte maximum at 50k after input-probe ledgers became resident-only. The frozen 1 GiB ceiling retains 48.49% headroom; an earlier 1,105,842,176-byte attempt exposed and led to removal of accidental one-shot ledger retention rather than a budget increase.
